item.title: Elaine Reichek’s Needlepoint Revolution
item.description: Also: Ro Reddick’s absurdist “Cold War Choir Practice,” Sofia Coppola’s portrait of Marc Jacobs, Paige Williams on music for spiritual uplift, and more.

item.title: Patricia Cornwell on Crime and Creativity
item.description: The prolific novelist—whose most famous character, the forensic pathologist Kay Scarpetta, is played by Nicole Kidman in a TV adaptation premièring in March—discusses a few of her perennial rereads.

item.title: The First Casualty of Trump’s War in Iran Was the Truth
item.description: The cruellest irony is that of a President who addresses the Iranian people in the language of liberation and then threatens freedom of the press back home.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Remnick

item.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
item.description: Queuing up is the new normal, especially when it comes to the T.S.A. Fifty minutes, two hours, half a day—how much time is a flight (or a cronut or a ticket to a Harry Styles show) worth?
item.dc:creator: ByZach Helfand

item.title: Between Sting and the Deep Blue Sea
item.description: The Police front man’s 2014 musical, “The Last Ship,” was inspired by his gritty working-class childhood in England. Now a revamped production—featuring Shaggy—is docking at the Metropolitan Opera.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Larson

item.title: Steve Zahn’s Father-Daughter Dance
item.description: At a Broadway studio, the actor busts a move with his daughter, Audrey, who also happens to be his inspiration (and co-star) for his new film, “She Dances.”
item.dc:creator: ByBrian Seibert

item.title: Remembering Calvin Tomkins, a Master of the Profile
item.description: For nearly seventy years, he captured the lives of modern artists forThe New Yorker.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Remnick

item.title: Robyn, on Her Own
item.description: The pop star brings motherhood and middle age to the dance floor.
item.dc:creator: ByJia Tolentino

item.title: Does A.I. Need a Constitution?
item.description: A new set of precepts is meant to make the chatbot Claude wise, decent, and safe. It also marks a striking transfer of public responsibility from constitutional government to private tech firms.
item.dc:creator: ByJill Lepore

item.title: Is Cuba Next?
item.description: Trump’s campaign to topple foreign adversaries encounters a battered but defiant regime.
item.dc:creator: ByJon Lee Anderson

item.title: The Last Generation
item.description: Life on a family farm.
item.dc:creator: Photography byBob Miller

item.title: Schools to Root for After Your Bracket Fails
item.description: To keep March Madness interesting, why not go with your mom’s alma mater? Or the college with a celebrity’s kid?
item.dc:creator: ByTalib Babb

item.title: “Floating”
item.description: After he left, I said to my friend, “I like him. Is he single?” My friend said he’d never mentioned a partner.
item.dc:creator: BySouvankham Thammavongsa

item.title: A Bingo Card for Spring in New York City
item.description: Do you have “UPS Guy in Shorts,” “Stoop Weirdo,” or “Park Teeming with Hinge Dates”?
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Flake

item.title: Under the Influence at the Whitney Biennial
item.description: How the artists in this year’s survey do or, more often, don’t acknowledge those who paved the way for them.
item.dc:creator: ByHilton Als

item.title: How Bad Is Plagiarism, Really?
item.description: From ancient Rome to the era of A.I., people have prized originality, but the line where influence ends and cribbing begins is notoriously blurry.
item.dc:creator: ByAnthony Lane

item.title: Engels in the Outfield
item.description: A radical history of the Mets insists that baseball can still be the people’s game.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “Kin,” “The Optimists,” “The Elusive Body,” and “Leaving Home.”

item.title: A “Baritenor” Soars in “Tristan und Isolde”
item.description: At the Met, Michael Spyres uses his broad vocal range to stunning effect, but Lise Davidsen loses power when she leaves her brilliant upper register.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Ross

item.title: “DTF St. Louis” Peers Into the Suburban Male Psyche
item.description: Jason Bateman excels as the Everyman, reeking of ennui and buried impulses, in the new HBO comic whodunnit, also with David Harbour and Linda Cardellini.
item.dc:creator: ByVinson Cunningham

item.title: “Coots”
item.description: “Were they coots, those dark birds with flashy / white bills, swimming together in circles / on winter waves?”
item.dc:creator: ByRosanna Warren

item.title: “Midnight in the Pain-Relief Aisle of CVS Thinking About ‘The Cloud of Unknowing’ ”
item.description: “Pain ricochets around my skull / like an aspirin commercial from the sixties.”
item.dc:creator: ByDonna Masini

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the March 30, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Wednesday, March 18, 2026
item.description: A beginner-friendly puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByCaitlin Reid
